name: GridItem
summary: Per-cell placement inside a `Grid` — `span` (column span), `rowSpan`, and `colStart`, each responsive (a breakpoint map compiles to CSS @media). Wrap a child in GridItem to make it occupy multiple tracks; bare Grid children remain valid 1×1 cells. Polymorphic via `as`.

props.name: colStart
props.description: 1-based column line to start at — `grid-column-start`. Responsive.

props.name: preset
props.description: One or many style-preset names registered via `definePreset()`

props.name: as
props.description: Polymorphic element override (renders this element/component instead of the default).

bestPractices: Use GridItem on an explicit track (e.g. `columns={12}`) for editor/preview or content/sidebar splits; make `span` responsive (`{ xs: 12, md: 8 }`) so it stacks on narrow screens.
Only wrap the children that need to span; leave 1×1 children bare — they place themselves.
`span`/`rowSpan` are spans (counts), `colStart` is a 1-based line index; combine `colStart` + `span` for precise placement.
GridItem is transparent to spacing — `gap`, `compact`, and `density` come from the parent Grid.
